title: Kaboom Crew gameplay v0 — input + bot AI + bombs + win/loss

stories.title: Keyboard input → GridMover.queuedDirection
stories.summary: Project-local `PlayerInputSystem` under `examples/kaboom-crew/src/` translates WASD + arrow keys into `GridMover.queuedDirection`. Keys are tracked via an internal pressed-set rebuilt from window keydown/keyup; the system writes whichever cardinal is currently held (right > up > left > down precedence to keep behaviour deterministic). Releases all keys on blur. Tagged via a new `PlayerControlled` (already in core.schema.json) component on the player entity to scope which mover the system drives. No bomb input yet — that lands in KABOOM-BOMB-PLACE.
verification: examples/kaboom-crew/src/player-input-system.ts registered through the project bootstrap
live playwright probe: pressing 'd' for 200ms moves the blue player one cell to the right (gx increments)
no global state — system stops listening when runtime.stop() runs
WASD + arrow keys translate to GridMover.queuedDirection; right > up > left > down precedence; 9 unit tests
no revision bump when direction is unchanged (ECS efficiency)
dispose() safe in non-DOM env; bootstrap registers PlayerInput + GridOccupancy + GridMovement under the static profile

stories.title: Bot AI v0 — wander + bomb-avoidance
stories.summary: Project-local `BotAISystem` reads `BotBrain` component (project schema fragment under examples/kaboom-crew/schemas) and writes `GridMover.queuedDirection`. Two behaviours: (1) wander — pick a random cardinal that is passable + not in the current danger map; (2) flee — when the current cell is in the danger map, prefer the direction with the lowest danger value. Danger map is derived from active bomb fuses + reachable blast cells through GridOccupancySystem; computed once per second (TimeBased component drives the cadence). Personality dial: `aggression` (0..1) — higher values reduce flee threshold.
verification: BotBrain schema validates inside examples/kaboom-crew/schemas/bot.schema.json
unit test: bot in a bomb's blast radius prefers the perpendicular escape cardinal
the seeded start scene runs for 30 seconds without the bot getting stuck on a wall
BotBrain { aggression, nextDecisionIn, lastDecision* } drives a 0.2s-tick decision loop in fixedUpdate
danger map built from active Bomb entities + range walk + soft-block shielding
flee picks a non-danger cardinal; wander biases to lastDecision (60 percent); aggression>0 + adjacent soft block triggers PlaceBombRequest
6 unit tests cover cooldown, wander, flee, aggression bomb-drop, dead-bot skip

stories.title: Place-bomb action: space key spawns a Bomb entity on the player's cell
stories.summary: Project-local `BombPlacementSystem` consumes a `PlaceBombRequest` transient component on the player. The transient is written by `PlayerInputSystem` on space key; system reads it, checks `BomberStats.activeBombs < BomberStats.maxBombs`, checks `GridOccupancySystem` for an existing bomb on the cell, then issues an `entity.create` command for a bomb prefab. Removes the transient at the end of the frame. Bots get their own `PlaceBombRequest` writer via BotAISystem so the same pipeline serves both.
verification: schemas/components for project-local Bomb + BomberStats + PlaceBombRequest under examples/kaboom-crew/schemas
bomb prefab examples/kaboom-crew/prefabs/bomb.prefab.json validates
unit test: BombPlacementSystem refuses when maxBombs cap is hit + when the cell already has a bomb
Space key fires edge-triggered PlaceBombRequest, BombPlacementSystem honours BomberStats.maxBombs cap + refuses stacking + increments activeBombs
5 unit tests cover the placement system
engine:check passes

stories.title: Bomb fuse → blast propagation → block destruction
stories.summary: `BombFuseSystem` decrements `Bomb.fuseRemaining` each fixedUpdate; at zero, it emits a `BlastEvent` transient + deletes the bomb entity. `BlastPropagationSystem` walks the four cardinals up to `Bomb.range` cells through `GridOccupancySystem`, stopping at the first cell that blocks blast (hard wall) and destroying any `soft-block` on the way. Chain reactions: a blast that overlaps another bomb's cell triggers that bomb early (fuse → 0). Visuals: each blast cell spawns a short-lived `BlastTile` entity carrying a `Tween` so the renderer paints a flash + fade — re-uses the existing Tween system.
verification: unit test: a 3-range bomb at (5,5) with a hard wall at (7,5) destroys soft blocks at (4,5), (6,5), (5,4..7) and stops at the wall
unit test: bomb-touches-bomb chain reaction fires within one fixed step
live: place bomb, fuse ticks, blast spawns 4 directional cells, soft blocks disappear, hard wall stays
BombFuseSystem ticks fuseRemaining + emits BlastEvent + deletes bomb + decrements activeBombs
Propagation: range walk in 4 cardinals, hard wall blocks + survives, soft block stops + is destroyed, chain reactions fire (target bomb fuse → 0)
BlastTile lifetime expires + removes tile; bombers on tile flip BomberStats.alive=false. 7 unit tests in bomb-blast-system.test.ts

stories.title: Power-up pickups: Bomb-up / Fire-up / Speed-up
stories.summary: Soft blocks roll a deterministic-by-cell pickup table on destruction (small `r = seededRng(cellKey)` so the same arena always seeds the same pickups). `PickupCollectSystem` detects player/bot entering a pickup-occupied cell, applies the effect to `BomberStats`, deletes the pickup. Visual: pickups use 3 distinct primitive shapes + colours; HUD shows the bomber's current stats via the existing HUD widget API.
verification: schemas/components/Pickup + BomberStats under examples/kaboom-crew/schemas
unit test: collecting Bomb-up increments BomberStats.maxBombs by 1
live: destroy a soft block, walk into the spawned pickup, see HUD widget update

stories.title: Damage + death: blast tiles kill bombers; round ends when only one is alive
stories.summary: BlastPropagationSystem additionally checks every `BomberStats`-carrying entity at the blast cell and sets `BomberStats.alive = false`. Project-local `RoundResolveSystem` watches surviving bombers each frame; when ≤ 1 remain alive (or 0 — draw), it flips a singleton `RoundState { phase: 'playing' | 'won' | 'lost' | 'draw', winnerId? }` and pauses every GridMover. The HUD watches `RoundState` and shows a win/loss banner.
verification: unit test: a bomber on a blast cell flips BomberStats.alive=false in the same step
RoundResolveSystem emits the correct phase for 1v0 / 0v0 / 1v1 (still playing) scenarios
HUD banner appears in the live game when the bot is blasted
bomber on a blast tile cell flips BomberStats.alive=false in the same step (covered by bomb-blast-system.test.ts)
RoundState + RoundResolveSystem moved to KABOOM-RESTART scope — damage flips alive here; the resolve step lands in the next story

stories.title: Round restart command — R key resets the scene + RoundState
stories.summary: Project-local input maps the `R` key to a `RoundRestartRequest` transient on a singleton control entity. `RoundResolveSystem` consumes it: applies the inverse-of-scene command chain (re-runs `scene.load` against examples/kaboom-crew/scenes/start.scene.json, or re-rolls a procedural seed). Lives entirely as data + commands so the same path serves agent replay scripts. No imperative renderer reset needed.
verification: live: end a round, press R, scene returns to initial layout, RoundState.phase resets to 'playing'
the engine recording captures the restart as a single scene.load command
RoundResolveSystem creates RoundState singleton, flips phase to won|lost|draw based on alive bombers, freezes GridMover on round end
RoundRestartRequest transient is ignored while phase='playing' + invokes onRestart when phase != 'playing'
bootstrap.attachUi binds KeyR globally → runtime.applyCommands([scene.load]) reloads start scene; bootstrap.resetRound is the canonical entry point. 6 unit tests

stories.title: HUD: round timer + bomber stats + win/loss banner
stories.summary: Three HUD widgets wired into the runtime.hud surface from S081: (a) topLeft — bomber stats (active bombs / range / speed icons + numbers); (b) topRight — minimap with player + bot + bomb markers (driven by createMinimapWidget); (c) center — win/loss banner driven by RoundState. Project queries the ECS each frame and pushes the data via runtime.hud.update — engine HUD primitive stays project-agnostic.
verification: live: HUD shows 1 / 1 / 4 (bombs/range/speed) at round start; updates when a Bomb-up is collected
live: minimap markers track the player + bot as they move
live: banner appears with the winning entity id after a blast kill

stories.title: Agent-friendly control surface: `runtime.kaboom = { gotoCell, placeBomb, status }`
stories.summary: Expose a small object on `runtime.kaboom` (mirrored onto `window.__agf.kaboom`) so an agent can drive Kaboom Crew without simulating keyboard events. v0 surface: (a) `gotoCell(entityId, gx, gz)` — sets a target cell that a project-local `AgentGotoSystem` walks toward each frame by writing `GridMover.queuedDirection` (one-step-at-a-time, respects walls + lane-assist); (b) `placeBomb(entityId)` — writes a `PlaceBombRequest` transient on the entity (same code path as space-key + bot AI); (c) `status()` — returns `{ round, players: [{ id, gx, gz, alive, activeBombs, stats }], bombs: [...], blastTiles: [...] }` so an agent can poll game state without parsing the full snapshot. Agent playtests + bug reproductions become one-liners.
verification: live probe: `__agf.kaboom.gotoCell('player.1', 5, 5)` then waiting 2 s moves player.1 from (1,1) toward (5,5) along a passable path
live probe: `__agf.kaboom.placeBomb('player.1')` spawns a Bomb entity in the same frame; cap respected
`__agf.kaboom.status()` returns a compact JSON snapshot suitable for curl piping
no existing keyboard / bot behaviour breaks — controls are additive

stories.title: BFS pathfinder so `gotoCell` walks around walls + soft blocks
stories.summary: v0 of `AgentGotoSystem` (KABOOM-AGENT-CONTROLS) only handles straight-line motion via `GridMover` lane-assist. This story adds a real BFS over the GridOccupancy index — each frame, the system recomputes the shortest path from current cell to target, queues the next cell as `queuedDirection`. Optionally caches the path until a wall changes (cheap invariant: occupancy revision counter). Treats soft blocks as walls today — bomb-it-down planning is a follow-up. Unblocks `__agf.kaboom.gotoCell` for traversal across the arena.
verification: unit test: BFS finds the 4-cell path from (1,1) to (1,5) around a soft-block wall at (1,3)
no path → system clears queuedDirection (player stops at current cell)
live probe: `__agf.kaboom.gotoCell('player.1', 13, 9)` traverses the arena around hard walls

stories.title: Robot playtest scenario — bot-vs-bot recording proves the round terminates
stories.summary: Drop a deterministic playtest under `examples/kaboom-crew/playtests/bot-vs-bot.json`: starts the scene, swaps the player's `PlayerControlled` for a second BotBrain, records commands + snapshots for 60 simulated seconds, asserts RoundState.phase eventually leaves 'playing'. Becomes the regression target for any AI / blast / round-state change.
verification: npm run playtest examples/kaboom-crew runs the scenario headlessly and exits 0 once RoundState.phase != 'playing'
the recording is reproducible — same seed produces a byte-identical snapshot at the end

followUps: AGENT-RENDERER-PROBE (engine-level) — `engine probe <projectDir>` + `runtime.renderer.inspect()` that returns a compact JSON dump of: (a) Three.js scene tree (Mesh / InstancedMesh / Light children + count + visibility), (b) BatchingSystem bucket records (key, capacity, live, members, lastWorld memo per entity), (c) MeshHandleRegistry handle → entityId map. The bug-hunt for the restart ghost players used this in ad-hoc form — `tmp-kaboom-probe.mjs` that reads `__agf.rendererInfo()`. A first-class tool means future render-side bugs are diagnosed in one curl, not in throwaway scripts. Live in a new engine sprint (likely engine-level under `M5` runtime diagnostics or as a new story under `E-56-ENGINE-DOCTOR-PROJECTDIR-SCORECARD`).

AGF-LOG-POLICY-DOC (engine S083) — write docs/diagnostics-policy.md capturing the severity scale + when-to-use-diagnostics-vs-console + structured-payload schema. Source: notes/logging-policy-thinking.md.

AGF-LOG-CI-GATE (engine S083) — scripts/check-no-console-log.mjs rejects raw console.log in engine/** + examples/**/src/**; wired into npm run repo:hygiene (already part of preflight). Allowlist via `// agf-allow:` markers.
AGF-LOG-AUDIT-ENGINE (engine S083) — sweep engine/** for raw console.log and replace with runtime.diagnostics.emit or console.warn/error with structured AGF_XXX codes. Mechanical, ~30 call sites.

AGF-LOG-LIFECYCLE-TRACES (engine S083) — scheduler.register / scene.load / bucket.acquire+release / meshHandle.acquire+release emit `info` diagnostics so an agent can reconstruct the boot/restart timeline from one snapshot. Would have surfaced the S82 ghost-player bug in one read.
AGF-LOG-PER-SYSTEM-DEBUG (engine S083+) — every system factory accepts `debug?: boolean`; when true emits per-tick state diff. `runtime.debug.<systemName>=true` flips it.

AGF-LOG-DOCTOR-DIAGNOSTICS (engine S083) — extend engine doctor with a Diagnostics: section: counts by (severity, code) since runtime start; flags any error or > 5 warnings of the same code as a recommendation.
AGF-LOG-BOOT-NOISE-BUDGET (engine S083) — preflight smoke asserts zero error/warning diagnostics after first paint for hello-3d + kaboom-crew. Regression gate on engine pipeline cleanliness.

AGF-LOG-ENTITY-RECREATED (engine S083) — scene.load detects when an entity id is removed AND re-added in the same command batch + emits AGF_ENTITY_RECREATED warning. Would have surfaced the BatchedMeshHandle-loss bug in one frame.
Audio (sound design pass) stays parked until MVP 2.
Procedural character generator stays parked — capsule placeholders cover MVP 0.
Multiplayer (server authority + client prediction + relevance filter) lives under the KABOOM-CREW-MVP-1 epic, not MVP 0.
Decal-on-grid (M27) + region-rule (M28) primitives are MVP-2-tier and not required for MVP 0.
notes: First sprint where Kaboom Crew is actually playable. Everything project-local lands under examples/kaboom-crew/src/ + examples/kaboom-crew/schemas/ — engine primitives stay frozen.
Round resolution + win/loss are ECS-data-driven (RoundState component) so an agent can flip phases via the command pipeline + drive end-screen UX from data.
